_purpose: Defines the structure for NEL gap reports. When Polsia processes an ancestor record and cannot find a matching NEL data file for any category, it generates a gap report using this schema. Gap reports accumulate and become build targets for Claude Code CLI.

ancestor_context.ancestor_id: The ancestor record ID that triggered the gap
ancestor_context.ancestor_race: Race as recorded in census if known

gaps.gap_type: One of: missing_trigger | missing_destination | missing_occupation | missing_route | missing_material_life | missing_community_texture | missing_regional_trigger | missing_template_situation | missing_research_guidance | missing_wage_data
gaps.severity: One of: HIGH (no match at all — narrative AI has no context) | MEDIUM (partial match — related data exists but not specific enough) | LOW (nice to have — existing data covers it approximately)
gaps.searched_for: What Polsia looked for (e.g., 'occupation: tanner', 'destination: Salt Lake City', 'trigger matching Scandinavian immigration 1880-1920')
gaps.nearest_match: The closest existing file that partially covers the gap, if any (e.g., 'blacksmith.json covers some metalworking context')
gaps.suggested_file: Suggested filename for the missing data (e.g., 'tanner.json', 'salt_lake_city.json')
gaps.frequency_note: If this gap has been seen before, note the count

gap_types_explained.missing_trigger: No core or regional trigger matches the ancestor's migration pattern. The narrative AI has no push/pull factors, no route, no historical context for why they moved.
gap_types_explained.missing_destination: The ancestor's destination city has no profile. The narrative AI can't describe neighborhoods, employers, or institutions at the destination.
gap_types_explained.missing_occupation: The ancestor's listed occupation has no profile. The narrative AI can't describe daily work, wages, conditions, or career trajectory.
gap_types_explained.missing_route: The trigger matched but the transportation route referenced doesn't have a file. The narrative AI can't describe the journey.
gap_types_explained.missing_material_life: No material life profile covers the ancestor's era + region combination. The narrative AI can't describe housing, food, clothing, or daily physical reality.
gap_types_explained.missing_community_texture: No community texture profile covers the ancestor's settled community. The narrative AI can't describe churches, schools, fraternal orders, or social fabric.
gap_types_explained.missing_regional_trigger: A national trigger matches but the ancestor's specific intra-state migration pattern isn't covered by a regional trigger. The national trigger is too broad.
gap_types_explained.missing_template_situation: An existing template should apply but no situation within it matches the ancestor's specific circumstances.
gap_types_explained.missing_research_guidance: A record gap was detected but no research guidance pattern covers how to resolve it.
gap_types_explained.missing_wage_data: The ancestor's occupation or era falls outside the existing wage table coverage.

priority_scoring.method: Gap priority = severity x frequency. A HIGH gap seen 10 times is the top build target. A LOW gap seen once can be deferred indefinitely.

thresholds.build_now: HIGH severity AND frequency >= 3, OR any severity AND frequency >= 10
thresholds.build_soon: MEDIUM severity AND frequency >= 5, OR HIGH severity AND frequency >= 1

output_instructions.where_to_output: Gap reports should be output as structured JSON appended to a gap_reports.jsonl file (one JSON object per line). Location: the repository's _gap_detection/ directory, or a designated output location configured by the deployment.
output_instructions.when_to_output: After every ancestor report generation that encounters at least one gap. Batch reporting is also acceptable — accumulate gaps during a session and output at end.
output_instructions.deduplication: Before outputting, check if an identical gap (same gap_type + same searched_for value) already exists in the accumulated reports. If so, increment frequency_note rather than creating a duplicate.

gaps.nearest_match: german_immigration.json covers similar era Northern European immigration but wrong ethnicity and different settlement patterns

gaps.searched_for: destination profile for Minneapolis or Minnesota farm settlement
gaps.nearest_match: minneapolis.json exists but covers urban destination, not rural farm settlement pattern
